title: Margetts – Enhancing Fund Strategy Through Targeted Macroeconomic Insight
gist: A promotional case study, not a macro research piece: it describes how Capital Economics supplies UK fund manager Margetts with bespoke monthly 2-3 page macro reports plus a conference call, which Margetts then folds into its own client communications. No market calls, forecasts, or data points are actually disclosed.

themes.theme: Research-as-a-service engagement model between Capital Economics and Margetts

insights.claim: Capital Economics runs a standing engagement with UK fund manager Margetts delivering monthly bespoke macro commentary in the form of a 2-3 page Word report followed by a conference call with the fund managers.
insights.speaker: Capital Economics
insights.so_what: Shows how boutique/mid-size fund managers outsource ongoing macro coverage to third-party research shops rather than building in-house macro teams, relevant when assessing research-vendor business models and buy-side cost structures.
insights.quote: For each topic, we produce a concise 2–3 page Word report summarising our key views and forecasts.

insights.claim: Margetts incorporates Capital Economics' externally produced reports directly into its own client-facing communications rather than only using them for internal discussion.
insights.speaker: Capital Economics
insights.so_what: A due-diligence flag for allocators: some fund managers' investor updates are partly white-labeled third-party macro views rather than fully proprietary house analysis, worth probing when evaluating a manager's research edge.
insights.quote: The reports are incorporated into the fund managers’ client-facing communications, reinforcing Margetts’ value proposition through timely, expert-backed insight.

insights.claim: The monthly topics covered for Margetts span a broad range of macro themes including geopolitical developments, monetary policy shifts, and market performance rather than a single specialty area.
insights.speaker: Capital Economics
insights.so_what: Indicates the breadth of coverage institutional clients expect from an external macro research subscription, a useful benchmark when comparing research vendors' scope.
insights.quote: from geopolitical developments to monetary policy shifts and market performance
